geometric_space.subtitle: Geometric Space
geometric_space.desc: Properties of shapes, plane & spatial figures (Junior High). Similarity, inscribed angles, and the Pythagorean theorem.

prep.name: Preparation: Assessment
prep.desc: Verify the version of your current mathematical OS.

jh.name: Junior High: Foundations
jh.desc: Solidify the logical thinking and spatial grasp required for High School math.

hs_1a.name: High School: Math I・A
hs_1a.desc: Basic tools directly connected to modern data science and design.

hs_2b.name: High School: Math II・B
hs_2b.desc: Advanced analysis tools for handling phenomenon transitions and energy accumulation.

hs_3c.name: High School: Math III・C
hs_3c.desc: The ultimate OS for manipulating space, dimensions, and limits to approach universal truths.

danger.title: Danger Zone
danger.wipe_desc: Delete all learning progress, XP, and rank. This action cannot be undone.

mastery.title: Mastery
mastery.desc: Displays the completion status of all units as a percentage. Clear 3 stages in all units to aim for 100% Mastery (OMEGA Rank).

score.title: Proficiency Score
score.desc: Your mathematical baseline based on initial assessment. Improve this precision by learning each unit.

what.title: What is Math-Tactix?
what.desc: A platform to understand high school math concepts visually. Move sliders and graphs to experience the meaning of equations hands-on.

modules_overview.title: Module Roles
modules_overview.quadratics: Quadratics — Visualize vertices, axes, and discriminants (exam essentials)
modules_overview.data: Data Analysis — Understand mean, variance, correlation, and regression with graphs
modules_overview.probability: Probability — Experience permutations, combinations, and conditional probability with animation
modules_overview.sets_logic: Sets & Logic — Learn Venn diagrams, necessary/sufficient conditions, and proof by contradiction visually
modules_overview.math_i_numbers: Numbers & Expressions — Step-by-step mastery of expansion, factoring, absolute values, and radicals
modules_overview.trig: Trigonometry — Interactive sine/cosine rules and surveying problems
modules_overview.calculus: Calculus — Manipulate tangent slopes and area calculations in real time
modules_overview.others: Others — Sequences, logarithms, vectors, complex numbers, matrices, and functions

gamification.title: Gamification Terms
gamification.rank: Rank — Progress from LEARNER to OPERATOR to ARCHITECT to OMEGA based on mastery
gamification.xp: XP — Experience points earned by clearing each level
gamification.omega: Omega Protocol — World-building flavor. The mathematical content is real high school math

recommended.title: Recommended Learning Path
recommended.desc: Start with 'Quadratics' or 'Numbers & Expressions'. Once you have the basics, move to 'Sets & Logic' or 'Data Analysis', then advance to 'Trigonometry' and 'Calculus'.

vis.title: Visualization Mission
vis.desc: Manipulate graphs and figures directly (e.g., vectors, data analysis) to achieve objectives. Emphasizes the link between 'intuition' and 'theory'.

logic.title: Logic Identification
logic.desc: Infer the hidden 'function (rule)' of a black box by observing output changes. Cultivates thinking similar to programming and AI training.

correlation.def: Relationship between two numerical data sets.

diff.def: Momentary rate of change or graph slope.

int.def: Accumulated total or area of small changes.

complex.def: Advanced number capable of describing planar rotation.

omega.name: OMEGA
omega.desc: 100% Mastery. One who has mastered all concepts.

operator.name: OPERATOR
operator.desc: 50%+ Mastery. Can use math as a tool.

overview.headline:
Math is not just
'Studying'.
overview.subheadline:
It is the only language that describes the world,
and the strongest weapon to hack the future.

logic.title: Construction of Logic
logic.desc: Cultivate the ability to find the rules behind phenomena and build logic, rather than just simple calculation.

reality.title: Connection to Reality
reality.desc: Present the use of mathematics in the real world, such as AI development, finance, and physics simulation, not just in textbooks.

tool.title: Professional Tool Perspective
tool.desc: Graduate from learning only to produce 'correct answers' and treat mathematics as a 'tool' to shape your own ideas.

quadratics.title: Quadratics
quadratics.subtitle: Mathematics I
quadratics.desc: Design parabolas and derive optimal solutions for parameters.

0.title: Secrets of Quadratics
0.desc: First, control the direction of the graph.

1.title: Decoding the Vertex
1.desc: y = (x - p)^2 + q is the code to find the vertex.
1.why_desc: Match the horizontal position that makes the inside of the parenthesis zero, and the height of the remaining number outside. Overlap the vertex at target (2.0, -1.0).

4.title: Tactical Objective
4.desc: There is always a limit (range) in the world. Mathematical tactics is about aiming for the best result (vertex) within that limited range.

5.title: Domain and Max/Min
5.desc: Consider the maximum and minimum values when the domain is restricted. Pay attention to the relationship between the endpoints and the vertex.

6.title: Quadratic Inequalities
6.desc: The solution to an inequality is determined by whether the graph is above or below the x-axis.

7.title: Parabola and Line Intersections
7.desc:
Move the line y = mx + k and observe how the number of intersection points changes.
This is equivalent to the number of real solutions of the system (discriminant).

8.title: Determining a Quadratic Function
8.desc:
When the vertex position and a point the graph passes through are known, the coefficient a can be determined.
Adjust the opening (a) so that the graph passes through point A.

9.title: Graph Translation
9.desc:
Translating y = f(x) by p in the x-direction and q in the y-direction gives y - q = f(x - p).
Move the original graph (blue) to overlap the target graph (red: y = 2(x-1)^2 + 1).

10.title: Coefficients and Graph Shape
10.desc:
Check how the signs of coefficients a, b, c and discriminant D affect the shape and position of the graph.
Determine all signs correctly.

11.title: Quadratic Inequality Solutions (Special Cases)
11.desc:
When discriminant D < 0, the parabola has no intersection with the x-axis.
Check whether the entire graph is above (all positive) or below (all negative).

12.title: Root Placement Problem
12.desc:
Visually understand the conditions for two distinct real roots to be greater than a value k.
Conditions: (1) D > 0, (2) axis > k, (3) f(k) > 0
12.success: Found the range satisfying the conditions!

13.title: Moving Domain and Max/Min
13.desc:
When the domain a <= x <= a+2 moves, observe how the x-values giving maximum/minimum change.
Move a so that the specified condition (minimum at vertex/left end/right end) is met.
13.mission_vertex: Range where minimum is at the vertex
13.mission_left: Range where minimum is at the left end
13.mission_right: Range where minimum is at the right end
13.hint: Focus on whether the vertex is inside or outside (left/right) the domain.

14.title: Final Exam
14.desc: Use the knowledge you have learned (completing the square, discriminant, max/min, root placement) to tackle practical exam problems.

tactics.q1_inst: Factor out coefficient 2 and identify the vertex.
tactics.q2_inst: Beware of sign errors when factoring out negative.
tactics.q3_inst: Fraction involved. Subtract the square of half correctly.
tactics.q4_inst: Find the number of intersection points between the quadratic function's graph and the x-axis.
tactics.q6_inst: Find the maximum value of the following quadratic function. (Domain: 0 <= x <= 3)
tactics.hint_discriminant: Hint: Check the sign of the discriminant D = b^2 - 4ac.
tactics.hint_graph: Hint: Sketch the graph and check the position of the axis.

data.title: Data Analysis
data.subtitle: Mathematics I
data.desc: Identify the truth behind data using correlation coefficients (r).

1.name: Signal Generation
1.desc: Generate baseline data. Plot 5+ points to achieve Correlation (r) > 0.90.
1.log_guide: Click to plot points and create a data trend.

2.name: Noise Removal
2.desc: Eliminate noise from data. Remove outliers to improve data precision.
2.log_guide: Click outliers that deviate from the trend to remove them.

3.name: Maximize Precision
3.desc: Aim for high synchronization. Maintain Correlation > 0.99 with 10+ points.
3.log_guide: Plot points densely to approximate a straight line.

4.name: Hypothesis Testing
4.desc: Is it a coincidence, or a meaningful difference? Determine significance at 5%.
4.log_guide: Perform coin tosses to check significance.

concepts.title: 01. Basic Concepts
concepts.correlation_body: An index indicating the relationship between two numerical datasets. For example, if 'Temperature' rises and 'Ice Cream Sales' rise, they have a Positive Correlation. If 'Rainfall' increases and 'Customer Count' decreases, they have a Negative Correlation.
concepts.importance_body: It is essential for predicting the future in the real world. Stock movements, disease progression, cosmic expansion. Everything is controlled by finding patterns in data relationships and predicting the next move.

theory.title: 02. Theoretical Model
theory.r_desc:
Correlation coefficient r ranges from -1 to +1.
Closer to +1 means strong positive correlation, closer to -1 means strong negative correlation, 0 means no relationship.

apps.title: 04. Applications
apps.finance_body: Used in 'Portfolio Theory' to analyze asset relationships and disperse risk. Combining assets with inverse movements reduces damage during crashes.
apps.ai_body: Used to select data strongly related to the prediction target when training AI. Removing irrelevant noise makes AI smarter.
apps.spurious_body: 'Ice cream sales' and 'drowning accidents' are strongly related, but one didn't cause the other (common cause: 'Temperature'). In data analysis, not being fooled by this 'spurious' relationship is key.

completion.msg: Data integrity confirmed. Your analysis result is approved.

probability.title: Probability
probability.subtitle: Mathematics A
probability.desc: Calculate uncertain events and compute future expected values.

1.mission: Move Mean (μ) to 2.0 to shift data center to the right.

2.mission: Decrease Standard Deviation (σ) to reduce data spread.

3.mission: Challenge the Monty Hall Problem and experience counter-intuitive probability.

4.mission: Observe the Law of Large Numbers. Increase trials and see distribution approach theoretical mean.

calculus.title: Calculus
calculus.subtitle: Mathematics II
calculus.desc: Master the moving world using laws of instantaneous change and accumulation.

1.name: Detect Change
1.desc: Capture 'movement'. Manipulate parameters and observe slope > 2.0.
1.log_guide: Move points on graph to create sharp change (slope).

2.name: Identify Vertex
2.desc: Find the moment change stops. Identify the point where slope becomes 0.
2.log_guide: Find the peak/valley (extremum). Change stops momentarily there.

3.name: Calculate Area
3.desc: Accumulate small changes. Achieve area under graph > 10.0.
3.log_guide: Use integration to calculate total amount enclosed by graph.

4.name: Real World App
4.desc: AI training, physics sim, economics. This math is used everywhere.

concepts.title: 01. Basic Concepts
concepts.diff_body: The world is always moving. How it changes 'right now'. That is differentiation. Speedometers differentiate position changes in real-time.
concepts.int_body: 'Accumulation' from the past. Summing up scattered pieces to calculate total distance, area, or energy. CT scans integrate body cross-sections to create 3D models.

theory.title: 02. Theoretical System
theory.derivative_desc:
Derivative f'(x): 'Momentum' or 'Slope' at a point. Captures momentary movement in infinitesimally small time.

Definite Integral ∫ f(x)dx: 'Area' between graph and horizontal axis. Sum of all small changes. Enables 'future prediction' and 'past analysis'.

apps.title: 04. Applications
apps.physics_body: Motion prediction. From falling apples to rocket trajectories, all movement is written in differential equations. Language to decode cosmic laws.
apps.ml_body: 'Learning' mechanism. AI finds the correct answer by descending the slope of its own errors (differentiation).
apps.econ_body: Profit maximization. Calculating 'how profit changes if we sell one more' to pinpoint the most profitable point.

completion.msg: Numeric optimization complete. Change laws mastered.

1.title: Definition of Derivatives
1.desc: Experience the limit definition of derivatives with h→0 slider

2.title: Power Rule
2.desc: Verify the power rule for x^n by changing n with a slider

3.title: Tangent Line Equation
3.desc: Move points on the graph and observe tangent line changes

4.title: Derivative Graph
4.desc: Compare f(x) and f'(x) graphs side by side

5.title: Sign Chart
5.desc: Determine increase/decrease from the sign of f'(x)

6.title: Local Max & Min
6.desc: Visually understand f'(x)=0 points and extrema

7.title: Graph Sketching
7.desc: Practice sketching graphs from sign charts

8.title: Integral Definition (Riemann Sum)
8.desc: Watch Riemann sums approach the area as partitions increase

9.title: Indefinite Integral
9.desc: Understand the relationship between integrand and antiderivative

10.title: Definite Integral
10.desc: Visualize the definite integral as area

11.title: Area Between Curves
11.desc: Calculate the area of regions bounded by two curves

12.title: Tangent Line Applications
12.desc: Tackle applied problems using tangent lines of quadratics

13.title: Comprehensive Extrema Practice
13.desc: Practice sign charts, extrema, and graph sketching for cubics

14.title: Volume of Revolution
14.desc: Use the disk method to find the volume of solids of revolution

page_ui.level_clear_desc: Let's move to the next level.

intro.title: Master Famous Angles
intro.desc: Experience the sensation of snapping 'like a magnet' to 30° and 45°.

quiz.success_desc: Burn this position into your brain.

trig_ratios.title: Trigonometric Ratios
trig_ratios.subtitle: Mathematics I
trig_ratios.desc: Master the fundamentals of surveying using right triangles and the unit circle.

1.title: Right Triangle and Definitions
1.desc: Verify that the ratios of sides in a right triangle (sine, cosine, tangent) are determined solely by the angle θ.

2.title: Unit Circle and Obtuse Angles
2.desc: Using a circle of radius 1 (unit circle), trigonometric ratios can be defined as coordinates (x, y). This allows us to consider trigonometric ratios for angles beyond 90° (obtuse angles).

3.title: Mutual Relations
3.desc: Visually understand the identity sin²θ + cos²θ = 1 as a right triangle on the unit circle.

4.title: Law of Sines
4.desc: In a general triangle, the ratio of each side to the sine of its opposite angle (a/sinA) is constant and equals the diameter of the circumscribed circle (2R).

5.title: Law of Cosines
5.desc: If two sides and the included angle are known, the remaining side can be found. Understand it as an extension of the Pythagorean theorem.

6.title: Area of a Triangle
6.desc: Express the base×height÷2 formula using trigonometric ratios. By understanding that h = b sinA, you can find the area of any triangle.

7.title: Inscribed Circle Radius
7.desc: Visually understand the relationship S = ½r(a+b+c) between the area S and inscribed circle radius r. Learn to calculate the radius from the area.

complex.title: Complex Plane
complex.subtitle: Mathematics C
complex.desc: Visualize new dimensions using imaginary rotation.

1.name: Imaginary Rotation
1.desc: Multiply real number 1 by i, move result to 'Imaginary Axis (Vertical)'.

2.name: Scale & Rotate
2.desc: Multiply by (1+i) to rotate 45 degrees and expand length.

3.name: Realize via Conjugate
3.desc: Manipulate current complex number to return result to 'Real Axis (Horizontal)'.

1.title: Complex Plane
1.desc: Basics of the Argand diagram, displaying a+bi as a point

2.title: Addition & Subtraction
2.desc: Vector sum and difference, parallelogram rule

5.title: Division
5.desc: Steps to rationalize using the conjugate

6.title: Modulus & Argument
6.desc: Definition of |z| and arg(z), correspondence with polar coordinates

7.title: Polar Form
7.desc: r(cosθ + i sinθ), vary r and θ with sliders

11.title: Loci of Points
11.desc: Figures like |z - a| = r, |z - a| = |z - b|

functions.title: Functions & Limits
functions.subtitle: Mathematics III
functions.desc: Master input/output logic and completely decode the black box.

1.name: Sector 1: Translation
1.hint: Signal shifted horizontally by fixed distance.

2.name: Sector 2: Scaling
2.hint: Wave height changing, slight offset occurring.

5.name: Sector 5: Exponential Explosion
5.hint: Growing at uncontrollable speed. Containment required.

6.title: Linear Function
6.desc: Manipulate slope and intercept of y = ax + b

7.title: Rational Function
7.desc: Explore asymptotes and shape of y = k/(x-h) + v

8.title: Radical Function
8.desc: Visualize the domain of y = a√(x-h) + v

9.title: Absolute Value Function
9.desc: Explore the piecewise graph of y = a|x-h| + v

10.title: Graph Transformations
10.desc: Function graph transformations via translation and reflection

13.title: Inverse Functions
13.desc: f⁻¹(x) and symmetry about y = x

14.title: Domain & Range
14.desc: Verify domains and ranges of various functions

15.title: Function Quiz
15.desc: Random quiz to test function understanding

1.name: Basic Addition
1.hint: A constant is always added to the input.

2.name: Linear Change
2.hint: Input is doubled, then 1 is subtracted.

4.name: Absolute Logic
4.hint: Negative values are converted to positive.

1.title: Observing Exponential Growth
1.desc: Experience the rapid growth of exponential functions

2.title: Definition of Logarithms
2.desc: Understand the correspondence: log_a M = p and a^p = M

3.title: Logarithmic Scale Compression
3.desc: Experience making huge numbers manageable with logs

4.title: Basic Log Properties
4.desc: Visually learn product, quotient, and power log rules

5.title: Change of Base Formula
5.desc: Understand log_a b = log_c b / log_c a

6.title: Common Log & Digit Count
6.desc: Master digit counting using log base 10

7.title: Logarithmic Equations
7.desc: Verify solutions of log_a f(x) = k on graphs

8.title: Logarithmic Inequalities
8.desc: Visualize solutions and regions of log inequalities

9.title: Log Graph Shifts
9.desc: Manipulate shifts of y = log_a(x-h) + k

10.title: Exp-Log Symmetry
10.desc: Confirm that y = a^x and y = log_a x are symmetric about y=x

11.title: Comparing Logarithms
11.desc: Learn techniques for comparing logarithmic values

12.title: Comprehensive Log Practice
12.desc: Solve problems using all logarithm rules

13.title: Log Comprehensive Quiz
13.desc: Random quiz to test all your log knowledge!

page_ui.level_clear_desc: Let's move to the next level.

matrices.title: Matrices
matrices.subtitle: Mathematics C
matrices.desc: Learn the mechanism of linear transformations that freely distort space.
matrices.level_1_desc: Manipulate matrix to rotate space 90 degrees counter-clockwise.
matrices.level_2_desc: Uniformly expand entire space to double size.

1.title: Matrix Basics
1.desc: Understand matrix definition, elements, rows, columns, and size

2.title: Matrix Addition & Subtraction
2.desc: Learn element-wise addition and subtraction of same-size matrices

3.title: Scalar Multiplication
3.desc: Multiply all elements of a matrix by a scalar

4.title: Matrix Product
4.desc: Visualize matrix multiplication via row-column dot products

6.title: Geometric Meaning of Determinant
6.desc: Visually understand that the determinant represents area scaling

7.title: Inverse Matrix
7.desc: Formula and steps for 2×2 inverse matrices

8.title: Identity & Zero Matrix
8.desc: Learn identity elements for multiplication and addition

9.title: Linear Transformation
9.desc: Observe real-time grid deformation by matrices

10.title: Rotation Matrix
10.desc: Learn the structure of rotation matrices and their angle relation

11.title: Systems of Equations
11.desc: Solve systems of equations in Ax = b form

12.title: Matrix Quiz
12.desc: Practice matrix calculations with random problems

page_ui.page_desc: Learn the mechanism of linear transformations that freely distort space.

sequences.title: Sequences
sequences.subtitle: Mathematics B
sequences.desc: Find continuity in patterns and predict future trends.

modal.success_desc: Sequence trend pattern confirmed. Proceeding to next step.

explanation.title: Explanation: Sequence Properties
explanation.arithmetic_desc: Change by adding fixed number. Draws straight line graph, steady growth.
explanation.geometric_desc: Change by multiplying fixed number. Slow at first, then jumps up rapidly (exponential growth).

1.title: Arithmetic Sequence Basics
1.desc: Manipulate first term and common difference to observe arithmetic graphs

2.title: Geometric Sequence Basics
2.desc: Manipulate first term and common ratio to observe geometric behavior

3.title: Arithmetic vs Geometric
3.desc: Quiz: determine whether a sequence is arithmetic or geometric

6.title: Geometric General Term
6.desc: Observe exponential growth of a_n = a_1 * r^(n-1)

7.title: Geometric Sum
7.desc: Verify partial sum formula with bar charts

8.title: Sigma Notation & Formulas
8.desc: Visually learn Σk, Σk², Σk³ formulas

9.title: Sigma Properties
9.desc: Verify constant multiple, distribution, and constant sum properties

10.title: Recurrence Relations
10.desc: Analyze a_{n+1} = pa_n + q type recurrences

11.title: Infinite Geometric Series
11.desc: Visually understand convergence condition |r| < 1 and limits

12.title: Difference Sequences
12.desc: Find the general term from difference sequences

13.title: Partial Fractions & Series
13.desc: Learn the mechanism of telescoping sums

14.title: Sequences Comprehensive Quiz
14.desc: Random quiz to test all your sequence knowledge!

page_ui.level_clear_desc: Let's move to the next level.

vectors.title: Vectors
vectors.subtitle: Mathematics C
vectors.desc: Manipulate 'direction' and 'magnitude' to define multi-dimensional space.

3.name: Visualization
3.log_guide: Align two vectors in same direction.

concepts.title: 01. Concept: Arrow Pointing to World
concepts.def_body: Not just a number, but a command: 'Go that way, this strongly'. Force, velocity, wind flow - all world-moving elements are vectors.
concepts.comp_body: Complex diagonal movement can be decomposed into simple 'horizontal' and 'vertical' movements. These are called 'components'.

theory.title: 02. Theory: Spatial Laws
theory.dot_desc: How much two arrows point in the 'same direction'. 0 if perpendicular. Used in game lighting calculations.
theory.cross_desc: Creates a new arrow perpendicular to both. Essential for finding rotational force (torque) or surface direction (normal).

viz.title: 03. Practice: Spatial Grasp
viz.log_guide: Adjust thrusters to maintain spatial balance.

apps.title: 04. Applications: 3D World
apps.cg_body: 3D game visuals are collections of vectors. Polygon direction and light direction determine brightness.
apps.phys_body: Gravity, wind, engine power. Summing all these forces as vectors determines object movement.
apps.nav_body: GPS and flight. Velocity vector combining 'direction' and 'speed' is used.

completion.msg: Vector operation mastered. You can freely manipulate 3D space.

1.title: Vector Basics
1.desc: Learn the definition of vectors as directed line segments

2.title: Component Form
2.desc: Express vectors using x and y components

3.title: Vector Addition & Subtraction
3.desc: Experience vector addition via the parallelogram rule

6.title: Dot Product
6.desc: Compute a·b = a1*b1 + a2*b2 and see the angle relation

7.title: Dot Product & Angle
7.desc: Find the angle using cos θ = a·b / (|a||b|)

10.title: Position Vector & Internal Division
10.desc: Learn internal division points using position vectors

11.title: Vectors Comprehensive Quiz
11.desc: Random quiz to test all your vector knowledge!

12.title: Space Vectors (3D)
12.desc: Explore dot product, magnitude, and angles in 3D space

13.title: Cross Product
13.desc: Learn cross product components, parallelogram area, and parallel condition in 3D

page_ui.level_clear_desc: Let's move to the next level.

balls_in_bins.title: Balls in Bins Simulation
balls_in_bins.subtitle: Randomly toss balls into {{bins}} bins (Law of Large Numbers)

trig.title: Trigonometry
trig.subtitle: Mathematics II
trig.desc: Synchronize circular motion with wave properties and analyze periodicity.

intro.title: Master Famous Angles
intro.desc: Experience the sensation of snapping 'like a magnet' to 30° and 45°.

quiz.success_desc: Burn this position into your brain.

25.title: Double Angle Formulas
25.desc: Derive and visualize sin2α, cos2α, tan2α from addition formulas

26.title: Half Angle Formulas
26.desc: Derive sin²(α/2), cos²(α/2), tan²(α/2) from double angle formulas

27.title: Triple Angle Formulas
27.desc: Derive and visualize sin3α, cos3α, tan3α from addition + double angle formulas

28.title: Product-Sum Conversion
28.desc: Convert between products and sums of trigonometric functions

29.title: Trig Synthesis
29.desc: Visualize a sinθ + b cosθ = R sin(θ+φ) synthesis formula

codex.title: Mathematical Codex
codex.header_desc: Fundamental explanations of mathematical concepts unlocked upon module mastery.

genesis.title: Genesis: The Language of Math
genesis.content: Mathematics is not merely a tool for calculation. It is the sole universal language for describing the mechanics of this world. By understanding the rules behind phenomena, we gain the power to control reality.

quadratics.title: Quadratics: Parabolas & Optimization
quadratics.content: The parabola drawn by a quadratic function exists everywhere in nature. From ball trajectories to satellite dishes. Finding the vertex is the very process of finding the 'optimal solution' in society.

trig.title: Trigonometry: Rhythm & Periodicity
trig.content: Everything in the universe vibrates. Sound, light, heartbeats. The sine wave describes all these periodicities. Learning trigonometry is nothing less than decoding the pulse of the universe.

vectors.title: Vectors: Mastery of Direction & Force
vectors.content: Position is relative, but direction and magnitude are absolute. Mastering vectors allows you to organize and uniquely determine the movement of objects and complex forces in multi-dimensional space.

data.title: Data Analysis: Order in Chaos
data.content: The world is full of informational noise. Correlation coefficients are the compass for extracting meaningful 'truth' from that chaos. Seeing the true connections between data is the first step in prediction.

calculus.title: Calculus: The Science of Change & Accumulation
calculus.content: There is no static moment. Differentiation captures the change 'right now', and integration quantifies the 'accumulation from the past'. Everything in the moving world is described through the lens of calculus.

progress.title: Learning Progress
progress.subtitle: Check mastery status for all modules.

data_analyst.name: Data Analyst
data_analyst.desc: Decoded the signal from the noise.

quiz_ace.name: Quiz Ace
quiz_ace.desc: Achieved a perfect score in the Proficiency Test.
